file_insights.purpose: Zod validation schemas for Auth module inputs/outputs
patterns: Modular schema organization with clear sections
Password validation with configurable complexity rules
Type inference from Zod schemas (z.infer<typeof schema>)
Utility functions for dynamic schema creation
complexity_indicators: Turkish character support in name validation

file_insights.purpose: Type definitions for Auth module (DTOs, tokens, sessions, password reset)

patterns_discovered.name: Modular Schema Organization
patterns_discovered.description: Schemas grouped into logical sections (Base, Registration, Login, Password Reset, Email Verification, Session, Settings, Response)
patterns_discovered.significance: Improves code maintainability and readability for large validation rule sets

patterns_discovered.name: Type Inference from Validation
patterns_discovered.description: Using z.infer<typeof schema> to automatically generate TypeScript types from Zod schemas
patterns_discovered.significance: Single source of truth for both validation and typing, reduces duplication

patterns_discovered.name: Password Complexity Factory Pattern
patterns_discovered.description: createPasswordSchema() function allows runtime configuration of password requirements
patterns_discovered.significance: Enables flexible security policies without code duplication

patterns_discovered.name: Cross-Field Validation
patterns_discovered.description: Using .refine() to validate password confirmation matches across multiple schemas
patterns_discovered.significance: Provides precise error messaging and field-specific error paths

patterns_discovered.name: Default Values with Constants
patterns_discovered.description: PASSWORD_DEFAULTS and PASSWORD_PATTERNS defined as constants at module top
patterns_discovered.significance: Centralizes configuration, simplifies maintenance and updates

patterns_discovered.name: Comprehensive Documentation
patterns_discovered.description: JSDoc comments for every schema with examples and type inference declarations
patterns_discovered.significance: Improves developer experience and API discoverability

gotchas_discovered.issue: Password schema complexity validation uses multiple refine() calls
gotchas_discovered.impact: Type narrowing can be lost, requiring 'as unknown as z.ZodString' casting in createPasswordSchema()
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider using superRefine() for better type preservation or creating base schemas for each requirement

gotchas_discovered.issue: Email schema transforms input (trim, toLowerCase) which happens during parse()
gotchas_discovered.impact: Validation errors may reference already-transformed values, potentially confusing users
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Document this behavior clearly in API responses

gotchas_discovered.issue: Phone number validation is optional but has complex regex
gotchas_discovered.impact: If phone becomes required later, users may get unexpected validation failures
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider standardizing phone number format library or abstracting to utility function

gotchas_discovered.issue: Settings schema has hardcoded default values (15m, 7d, 8, etc.)
gotchas_discovered.impact: Changes to defaults require code modifications; no runtime configuration loading
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider loading defaults from environment or configuration files

gotchas_discovered.issue: Token validation uses max length of 256 characters
gotchas_discovered.impact: May be insufficient for tokens with multiple signatures or claims
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify this limit aligns with actual token generation implementation

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created comprehensive Auth module structure with validation schemas and type definitions
deliverables_completed: Created auth.schema.ts with 10+ Zod validation schemas covering all Auth operations
Created auth.types.ts with comprehensive TypeScript type definitions
Created module.json with module metadata and configuration
Implemented password complexity validation with configurable rules
Added utility functions for dynamic schema generation and validation

recommendations.recommendation: Add unit tests for schema validation, especially edge cases like password complexity, cross-field validation, and internationalized names
recommendations.rationale: Validation schemas are critical security components; comprehensive tests prevent regressions

recommendations.recommendation: Create a centralized error formatter to translate Zod validation errors into user-friendly messages
recommendations.rationale: Raw Zod errors expose validation implementation details; user-facing errors should be abstracted

recommendations.recommendation: Extract hardcoded defaults (PASSWORD_DEFAULTS, auth expiry times) to environment variables or config files
recommendations.rationale: Enables deployment-specific security policies without code changes

recommendations.recommendation: Resolve type casting issues in createPasswordSchema() using superRefine() or alternative approach
recommendations.rationale: Improves type safety and reduces 'as unknown' casts that can hide type errors

recommendations.recommendation: Add architectural documentation explaining validation flow and how schemas integrate with auth service implementation
recommendations.rationale: Helps future developers understand design decisions and integration points

recommendations.recommendation: Validate that phone regex pattern aligns with actual supported formats and consider i18n for phone validation
recommendations.rationale: Phone validation currently has minimal documentation; inconsistency could cause user confusion

recommendations.recommendation: Review token length limit (256 chars) against actual token generation implementation to ensure no truncation
recommendations.rationale: Misaligned limits could cause authentication failures in production
